content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 16, 1993

Thomas S. Knox
Knox, Lemmon, Brady, et al.
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 1170
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re:  Your Request for Assistance
     Our File No. I-93-236

Dear Mr. Knox:

     This is in response to your request for assistance under the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").1/  Since you are merely seeking confirmation of your
understanding of the provisions of the Act, and not inquiring
about a specific decision or provision before you, your request is being
treated as a request for informal assistance.2/  In addition, this
assistance is limited to the provisions of the Act.  We cannot
provide advice about other conflict-of-interest laws, such as
Government Code Section 1090.  You should contact the Attorney
General's office for advice under Section 1090.

     In your letter you stated that the California-Nevada Super
Speed Ground Transportation Commission (the "Commission") is a
nonprofit corporation operating under the laws of the states of
Nevada and California.  The Commission is developing a plan to
build a high speed railroad between Las Vegas and Southern
California.  The Commission is considering raising revenue by
soliciting the business community to contribute funds to the
Commission.  It is conceivable that some businesses or individuals
responding to such a solicitation may, in the future, bid to
become providers of goods or services to the Commission.


1/  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000 et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/  Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento, CA  95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

File No. I-93-236
Page Two


It is your understanding that the Act only pertains to
conflicts of interest on the part of officials who have received
personal gifts (or hold some financial interest) in a business
which may be affected by the official's decision. You wish to
confirm that understanding.

Generally, the conflict-of-interest provisions pertain to
economic interests of public officials. (Section 87100 et seq.)
The receipt of donations or contributions by the Commission would
not normally be income or gifts to the officials within the
Commission, with the following exception.

If the funds donated by an individual or entity were
earmarked for use by a specific official within the Commission,
the contribution would be a source of a gift to the official. If
the value of the gift was $250 or more, the official may have a
conflict of interest which would prevent the official from
participating in any decision affecting the source of the gift.

In certain circumstances a gift used by a public official
could be structured in such a way that the gift was in fact made
to the agency rather than the public official. (In re Stone
(1977) 3 FPPC Ops. 52, copy enclosed.) For the Stone exception to
apply, all of the following must occur:

1. The donor must intend to donate the gift to the
public entity and not to the official;

2. The public entity must exercise substantial
control over use of the gift;

3. The gift must be generally available to agency
personnel in connection with agency business without
regard to official status. The donor cannot have
limited the use of the gift to specific persons or high
level employees; and

4. The making and use of the gift must be formal-
ized in a written public record of the public entity
which embodies the standards set forth above.

Accordingly, except for gifts earmarked for specific
officials, donations received by the Commission from an individual
or entity, are donations to the Commission, not to the public
officials within the Commission.

File No. I-93-236
Page Three


I trust this sufficiently confirms your understanding of the
conflict-of-interests provision. If you have additional
questions or concerns, please feel free to contact the
Commission's Legal Division at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Jeff Marschner
General Counsel

[signature]

By: Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Legal Division

JM/JET/jt
Enclosure
